parsed_review.summary: CP00-420 is a faithful descriptor-only continuation of the RP13.P09.M07 Test And Golden Case Set slice (10 units, S02-S11, risk A). The manifest plan_binding_snapshot matches the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry exactly and next-pack-queue.json (live_latest_pack_id CP00-419) confirms CP00-420 is the correct next pack, so deviation_from_plan:false is accurate. The CP420 pack binding, no-write attestation, requirements, descriptor projection, case-set builder, validators, contract embedding, validate script, README entry, and tests are mutually consistent. The case-set rows hard-code descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / customer_safe_errors_only:true / raw_payload_included:false with guard fields ordered after spreads (cannot be overridden), and validators re-check every guard plus the authority boundary and handoff target. command-evidence.json records all required validation commands (npm test, npm run validate, build, git diff --check, Hermes H13 control-plane validates) at exit_code 0. The authority boundary is preserved end-to-end: production_ready:false and runtime_ready:false, a descriptor-scoped production_ready_flag, Claude as read-only reviewer (not final approver), enterprise trust not claimed from local validation, and Hermes present only as deterministic review-packet/receipt metadata (no product runtime). forbidden_review_evidence covers all CP178+ hardening categories and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one non-blocking P3 bookkeeping observation in the changed-file-scope ledger. The pack supports its descriptor-verified claim and is safe to close.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists the pack's own evidence dir as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-420/ and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (line 130) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 135). The cp00-420/ directory is the pack's own required-artifact directory (it holds manifest.json and command-evidence.json, both validated by the test suite via readFileSync), so labeling it 'unrelated' is internally inconsistent. Relatedly, manifest.worktree_transition_policy.active_pack_required_untracked_files is [] even though the pack's required artifacts are untracked. This is a categorization/labeling quirk in hardening bookkeeping artifacts only; it does not affect implementation correctness, the descriptor-only boundary, no-write/no-real-data claims, validation outcomes, or the authority boundary.
findings.recommendation: In a future generator pass, classify the pack's own evidence directory as pack-scoped evidence rather than 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved', and make active_pack_required_untracked_files reflect the untracked required artifacts. No action required for this closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Non-blocking. Cosmetic scope-ledger inconsistency with no effect on the production_ready/descriptor claim or boundaries. Safe to proceed with CP00-420 closeout.
